derivation: Chen ER notation: weak entity set = double rectangle; identifying relationship = double diamond (C is the trap); underline = primary key attribute.
An album is a real-world thing you store data about, i.e. an entity (type). Attribute would be title/year; instance would be one specific album.

A specific record / one particular occurrence of an entity type is an instance. Relation/Table = the whole set, Column = attribute. Note: shipped explanation is wrong (it describes an Oracle 'database instance' = background processes) but the keyed option B is still correct.
AMAT = Hit time + Miss rate * Miss penalty (Hennessy & Patterson). Penalty is added, and it is a product not a quotient.

S = G*e^(-2G), dS/dG = 0 at G = 0.5, so S_max = 0.5*e^-1 = 0.1839 = 18.4%. Exact 18.4% is NOT among the options: 18.8% (A) is not a correct rounding of anything, while 18% (B) is 18.4% rounded to the nearest percent, so B is the only defensible option. C (36.8%) is slotted ALOHA = 1/e. Item is imprecise (should list 18.4%) but answerable; keeping defective=false since B is uniquely defensible.
Compiled and ran with g++: NULL is integer constant 0, so if (NULL) is false and the else branch prints. Output: Hii World.
Compiled and ran: > binds tighter than &&, so A && (10 > 20) = false; then A = (B || C == 20) is an assignment of (10 || 1) = 1, which is truthy. Output: PQR.
Compiled and ran: i = j++ leaves j odd (5,7,9,11) before the j==8 test, so the continue never fires and the extra j++ makes j step 4,6,8,10. Output: 4 6 8 10 on separate lines.
Compiled and ran: plain counted loop i = 1..5 printing each value plus a space. Output: 1 2 3 4 5 (with trailing space).
Compiled and ran: the stray semicolon makes the for body empty, so the loop exits with i == 10 and the following brace block prints once. Output: 10.

AMBIGUOUS ITEM. C++ has three iteration statements: for, while, do-while (=> C). Counting the C++11 range-based for gives four, but the shipped explanation instead counts 'nested' as a fourth type, which is not a loop type at all - a nested loop is just a loop inside a loop. The keyed answer A (4) is what several Indian MCQ banks say (the option order 4/2/3/1 matches that bank), so it may well be what the exam expects, but it is not defensible as written. Flagging rather than recommending a key flip, since matching the source key is likely safer for the exam.
break exits the enclosing loop immediately, cancelling all remaining iterations. B describes continue; C describes exit()/return from main; D is false.
Compiled and ran with an instrumented counter: every iteration takes continue (x < 5) or break (x >= 5), so the printf after the if/else is unreachable. Count = 0.
Compiled and ran with an instrumented counter: j takes odd values 1,3,...,249 (last <= 250), then exits at 251. (249-1)/2 + 1 = 125 iterations.
& is bitwise AND. The logical operators in C/C++ are && , || and !.
